issues.title: [Bug]: Permission denied and could not request permission from user error
issues.next_action_reason: Community bug, 57d unanswered; need CLI version + approval mode + session report to separate CLI approval bug from skill behavior.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the report @nakultt. The message “Permission denied and could not request permission from user” is emitted by the Copilot CLI’s command-approval layer rather than by the skill itself — it fires when a chained/compound command (here `dotnet new ... && cd ... && Get-ChildItem ...`) can’t be auto-approved and there’s no interactive TTY to prompt on. To help us pin it down, could you share: (1) your Copilot CLI version (`copilot --version`), (2) whether you ran with `--allow-all-tools` or a specific approval mode, and (3) the session report if you have one? If it only fails on the compound `&&` command, running the steps individually or pre-approving shell should unblock you in the meantime.
